ops.text: PRE-REGISTRATION E34 (Wave 0; triage half only — the decision-task half stays gated on E30 completion per the plan). Hypothesis: test-time compute over the incumbent v6 triage judge — self-consistency majority voting or generate-then-verify — raises agreement with the blind opus auditor vs single-pass. Data: auditor-gold v1: DEV(100) for selection, TEST(241) scored EXACTLY ONCE at close; TRAIN untouched. Arms: SC-3 and SC-7 (temperature-diversified samples of the v6 judge; majority on binary is_steering, plurality on category, ties → single-pass verdict) and GenRM best-of-8 (8 sampled verdicts; an opus verifier scores each rationale and picks — verifier is one model doing comparative scoring, not a quality judgment panel, so PoLL is not triggered; if any arm instead needs an absolute LLM quality judgment it runs PoLL 3-jury per E30.3). Baseline: single-pass v6, same rows, same rendering. Selection funnel (dev only): all three arms + baseline on a FIXED dev-50 subset; the single best arm by binary agreement advances directly to test (no full-dev pass — cost discipline). Test (once): winner arm + baseline on all 241. DECISION RULE: exact McNemar p<0.05 AND ≥3 points absolute binary-agreement gain; CO-PRIMARY: per-row cost multiplier vs single-pass — a statistically-winning arm costing >5x is reported to synthesis as 'wins-but-expensive', never auto-adopted. Scoring model: sonnet (v6 pipeline tier); GenRM verifier: opus. Spend: $20 HARD CAP, running ledger, abort at $18 projected. Harness ~/.cc-steer/experiments/E34/, own venv, cc-steer from git+file://…@main. VERIFY: recompute McNemar/agreement + cost multiplier from persisted per-row JSONL (every vote/sample persisted), audit test-touched-once, vote-aggregation determinism (majority/plurality/tie rules as registered), ledger ≤ cap. No artifact ships.
